concept: Laugh / Express Amusement Vocally / Make Sound of Mirth
description: To express amusement, joy, or mirth through characteristic vocal sounds—the involuntary release of breath in rhythmic bursts that signals delight, sometimes gentle, sometimes roaring. 'Hola' is the body's spontaneous celebration of the funny, the absurd, the joyful.
sound_symbolism: The word releases like laughter itself. 'Ho' breathes out with open vowel, the expulsion of air that begins a laugh. Then 'la' flows freely with liquid release, laughter continuing, rolling onward.
grammatical_notes: As verb: 'mi hola' (I laugh). As noun: 'lau hola' (great laughter). Can specify cause: 'mi lo themo hola' (I laugh at something). Related to kela (rejoice) but specifically the vocal/physical expression.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Laughter releases attachment—we cannot laugh and cling simultaneously. Humor reveals the absurdity of taking things too seriously.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Shared laughter builds connection instantly—people who laugh together find it harder to harm each other.
